summary: Services section integrated high-fidelity videos but lacks architectural unity and data-driven rendering. Redundancy between Services.tsx and ServiceCard.tsx detected.

findings.title: Data-UI Coupling
findings.description: Services.tsx hardcodes video paths and descriptions that already exist in src/data/services.ts. This leads to out-of-sync content and maintenance debt.
findings.recommendation: Refactor Services.tsx to map over the services data array.

findings.title: Component Inconsistency
findings.description: The ServiceCard component is bypassed in the main Services grid in favor of ad-hoc motion.div elements. This fragments the design system.
findings.recommendation: Unify card rendering logic into a single high-fidelity ServiceCard component supporting multiple bento-grid variants.

findings.title: Luxury Profile Alignment
findings.description: The CTA banner uses a static background image which contrasts too sharply with the video-driven grid above. Typography spacing in CTA needs 'Quiet Luxury' refinement (increased tracking, subtle letter-spacing).
findings.recommendation: Apply cinematic video background to CTA or use a more abstract, premium gradient-mesh with blueprint overlays.

findings.title: Video Resource Management
findings.description: Multiple auto-playing videos are loaded regardless of scroll position.
findings.recommendation: Implement Intersection Observer to play/pause videos based on visibility.
